data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was an endogenous political revolution where subject populations, empowered by the diffusion of iron-working technology which bypassed palatial control of bronze, deliberately dismantled the bureaucratic apparatus to escape tribute obligations, with the 'Sea Peoples' representing displaced refugees of this internal fracturing rather than an external invading army.", "mechanism": "Technological democratization enabling class warfare and bureaucratic dissolution", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Levantine city-states"], "excludes": ["Egypt", "Assyria"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Evidence that iron production remained strictly under palatial monopoly until after 1100 BC", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "A more atypical synthesis combining internal class struggle with the technological shift to iron, reinterpreting the Sea Peoples as a symptom rather than the primary cause."}
